description_wiki: Southern Exposure may refer to:

description: American folklorist
description_wiki: Margaret Anne "Peggy" Bulger is a folklorist and served as the director of the American Folklife Center at the Library of Congress from 1999 to 2011, when she moved to Florida to continue work on personal projects.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Pennsylvania, officially the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, is a U.S. state located in the Mid-Atlantic, Northeastern, Appalachian, and Great Lakes regions of the United States.

description: Author, folklorist, anti-Ku Klux Klan crusader (1916-2011)
description_wiki: William Stetson Kennedy (October 5, 1916 – August 27, 2011) was an American author, folklorist and human rights activist.

description: American educator
description_wiki: Myles Falls Horton (July 9, 1905 – January 19, 1990) was an American educator and political activist.

description: cultural region in the southeastern and south-central United States
description_wiki: The Southern United States (sometimes Dixie, also referred to as the Southern States, the American South, the Southland, Dixieland, or simply the South) is one of the four census regions  of the United States as defined by the U.S. Census Bureau.

description: city in Duval County, and largest city in State of Florida, United States
description_wiki: Jacksonville (US:  , JAK-sən-vil; colloquially nicknamed Jax) is the most populous city proper in the U.S. state of Florida, located on the Atlantic coast of northeastern Florida.

description: former country in North America formed by the Southern United States during the American Civil War (1861-1865)
description_wiki: The Confederate States of America (CSA), also known as the Confederate States (C.S.), the Confederacy, or the South, was an unrecognized breakaway republic in the Southern United States from 1861 to 1865.

aliases: National Association of Daughters of the Confederacy
description: lineage society based in Richmond, Virginia, USA
description_wiki: The United Daughters of the Confederacy (UDC) is an American neo-Confederate hereditary association for female descendants of Confederate Civil War soldiers engaging in the commemoration of these ancestors, the funding of monuments to them, and the promotion of the pseudohistorical Lost Cause ideology and corresponding white supremacy.

description: county seat of Bulloch County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Statesboro is the most populous city in and the county seat of Bulloch County, Georgia, United States.

description: consolidated city-county in Bibb County, Georgia, United States of America
description_wiki: Macon ( MAY-kən), officially Macon–Bibb County, is a consolidated city-county in Georgia, United States.

description: city in Charlotte County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Punta Gorda (; English: Fat Point) is a city located in Southwest Florida, United States.

description: county seat of Buncombe County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Asheville ( ASH-vill) is a city in Buncombe County, North Carolina, United States.

description: county in Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Bullock County is a county of the U.S. state of Alabama.

description: English novelist and social critic (1812–1870)
description_wiki: Charles John Huffam Dickens ( ; 7 February 1812 – 9 June 1870) was an English writer and journalist.

description: seacoast town in Plymouth County, Massachusetts
description_wiki: Scituate ( ) is a seacoast town in Plymouth County, Massachusetts, United States, on the South Shore, midway between Boston and Plymouth.

description: public research university in Gainesville, Florida, United States
description_wiki: The University of Florida (Florida or UF) is a public land-grant research university in Gainesville, Florida, United States.

capital city of Georgia, United States

description: terrestrial continent located in north-western Eurasia
description_wiki: Europe is a continent located entirely in the Northern Hemisphere and mostly in the Eastern Hemisphere.

description: northernmost region of the African continent
description_wiki: North Africa, also known as Northern Africa, is a region encompassing the northern portion of the African continent.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.

description: American politician (1900–1989)
description_wiki: Claude Denson Pepper (September 8, 1900 – May 30, 1989) was an American politician of the Democratic Party.

description: American politician (1913–2007)
description_wiki: George Armistead Smathers (November 14, 1913 – January 20, 2007) was an American lawyer and politician from the state of Florida who served in both chambers of the United States Congress, the United States House of Representatives from 1947 to 1951 and the United States Senate from 1951 to 1969.

U.S. states of North Carolina and South Carolina
description_wiki: The Carolinas (informally Carolina) are the U.S. states of North Carolina and South Carolina considered together as a historical or cultural entity.
